ast.name: addOption
ast.source: Render method description and parameters list
ast.target: Render method description and parameters list

ast.name: addOption
ast.source: Render method description, parameters list, and return
ast.target: Render method description, parameters list, and return

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Determine how you'd like to have user script intellisense render. Note values will not render if not in the script.
ast.target: Determine how you'd like to have user script intellisense render. Note values will not render if not in the script.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: If you like this Plugin, consider donating to support continued development.
ast.target: If you like this Plugin, consider donating to support continued development.

ast.name: description
ast.source: This module exposes the app instance. Prefer to use this over the global app instance.
ast.target: This module exposes the app instance. Prefer to use this over the global app instance.

ast.name: description
ast.source: This module exposes custom made scripts, written by yourself within the script file folder location
ast.target: This module exposes custom made scripts, written by yourself within the script file folder location

ast.name: description
ast.source: This module contains every internal function related to dates.
ast.target: This module contains every internal function related to dates.

ast.name: description
ast.source: The format for the date. Defaults to `"YYYY-MM-DD"`. Refer to [format reference](https://momentjs.com/docs/#/displaying/format/).
ast.target: The format for the date. Defaults to `"YYYY-MM-DD"`. Refer to [format reference](https://momentjs.com/docs/#/displaying/format/).

ast.name: description
ast.source: Duration to offset the date from. If a number is provided, duration will be added to the date in days. You can also specify the offset as a string using the ISO 8601 format.
ast.target: Duration to offset the date from. If a number is provided, duration will be added to the date in days. You can also specify the offset as a string using the ISO 8601 format.

ast.name: description
ast.source: The date referential, e.g. set this to the note's title.
ast.target: The date referential, e.g. set this to the note's title.

ast.name: description
ast.source: The format for the reference date. Refer to [format reference](https://momentjs.com/docs/#/displaying/format/).
ast.target: The format for the reference date. Refer to [format reference](https://momentjs.com/docs/#/displaying/format/).

ast.name: description
ast.source: Week day number. If the locale assigns Monday as the first day of the week, `0` will be Monday, `-7` will be last week's day.
ast.target: Week day number. If the locale assigns Monday as the first day of the week, `0` will be Monday, `-7` will be last week's day.

ast.name: description
ast.source: This module contains every internal function related to files.
ast.target: This module contains every internal function related to files.

ast.name: description
ast.source: The filename of the new file, defaults to "Untitled".
ast.target: The filename of the new file, defaults to "Untitled".

ast.name: description
ast.source: Whether to open or not the newly created file. Warning: if you use this option, since commands are executed asynchronously, the file can be opened first and then other commands are appended to that new file and not the previous file.
ast.target: Whether to open or not the newly created file. Warning: if you use this option, since commands are executed asynchronously, the file can be opened first and then other commands are appended to that new file and not the previous file.

ast.name: description
ast.source: The folder to put the new file in, defaults to Obsidian's default location. If you want the file to appear in a different folder, specify it with `"PATH/TO/FOLDERNAME"` or `tp.app.vault.getAbstractFileByPath("PATH/TO/FOLDERNAME")`.
ast.target: The folder to put the new file in, defaults to Obsidian's default location. If you want the file to appear in a different folder, specify it with `"PATH/TO/FOLDERNAME"` or `tp.app.vault.getAbstractFileByPath("PATH/TO/FOLDERNAME")`.

ast.name: description
ast.source: The format for the date. Defaults to `"YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm"`. Refer to [format reference](https://momentjs.com/docs/#/displaying/format/).
ast.target: The format for the date. Defaults to `"YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm"`. Refer to [format reference](https://momentjs.com/docs/#/displaying/format/).

ast.name: description
ast.source:
The order of the different cursors jump, e.g. it will jump from 1 to 2 to 3, and so on.
If you specify multiple tp.file.cursor with the same order, the editor will switch to multi-cursor.
ast.target:
The order of the different cursors jump, e.g. it will jump from 1 to 2 to 3, and so on.
If you specify multiple tp.file.cursor with the same order, the editor will switch to multi-cursor.

ast.name: description
ast.source: Appends some content after the active cursor in the file.
ast.target: Appends some content after the active cursor in the file.

ast.name: description
ast.source: The content to append after the active cursor.
ast.target: The content to append after the active cursor.

ast.name: description
ast.source: Check to see if a file exists by it's file path. The full path to the file, relative to the Vault and containing the extension, must be provided.
ast.target: Check to see if a file exists by it's file path. The full path to the file, relative to the Vault and containing the extension, must be provided.

ast.name: description
ast.source: The full file path of the file we want to check existence for.
ast.target: The full file path of the file we want to check existence for.

ast.name: description
ast.source: Search for a file and returns its `TFile` instance.
ast.target: Search for a file and returns its `TFile` instance.

ast.name: description
ast.source: The filename we want to search and resolve as a `TFile`.
ast.target: The filename we want to search and resolve as a `TFile`.

ast.name: description
ast.source: If set to `true`, returns the vault-absolute path of the folder. If `false`, only returns the basename of the folder (the last part). Defaults to `false`.
ast.target: If set to `true`, returns the vault-absolute path of the folder. If `false`, only returns the basename of the folder (the last part). Defaults to `false`.

ast.name: description
ast.source: The link to the file to include, e.g. `"[[MyFile]]"`, or a TFile object. Also supports sections or blocks inclusions.
ast.target: The link to the file to include, e.g. `"[[MyFile]]"`, or a TFile object. Also supports sections or blocks inclusions.

ast.name: description
ast.source: Retrieves the file's last modification date.
ast.target: Retrieves the file's last modification date.

ast.name: description
ast.source: Moves the file to the desired vault location.
ast.target: Moves the file to the desired vault location.

ast.name: description
ast.source: The new vault relative path of the file, without the file extension. Note: the new path needs to include the folder and the filename, e.g. `"/Notes/MyNote"`.
ast.target: The new vault relative path of the file, without the file extension. Note: the new path needs to include the folder and the filename, e.g. `"/Notes/MyNote"`.

ast.name: description
ast.source: The file to move, defaults to the current file.
ast.target: The file to move, defaults to the current file.

ast.name: description
ast.source: If set to `true`, only retrieves the vault's relative path.
ast.target: If set to `true`, only retrieves the vault's relative path.

ast.name: description
ast.source: Renames the file (keeps the same file extension).
ast.target: Renames the file (keeps the same file extension).

ast.name: description
ast.source: Retrieves the active file's text selection.
ast.target: Retrieves the active file's text selection.

ast.name: description
ast.source: Retrieves the file's tags (array of string).
ast.target: Retrieves the file's tags (array of string).

ast.name: description
ast.source: This modules exposes all the frontmatter variables of a file as variables.
ast.target: This modules exposes all the frontmatter variables of a file as variables.

ast.name: description
ast.source: This module exposes all the functions and classes from the Obsidian API.
ast.target: This module exposes all the functions and classes from the Obsidian API.

ast.name: description
ast.source: Spawns a prompt modal and returns the user's input.
ast.target: Spawns a prompt modal and returns the user's input.

ast.name: description
ast.source: Text placed above the input field.
ast.target: Text placed above the input field.

ast.name: description
ast.source: A default value for the input field.
ast.target: A default value for the input field.

ast.name: description
ast.source: Throws an error if the prompt is canceled, instead of returning a `null` value.
ast.target: Throws an error if the prompt is canceled, instead of returning a `null` value.

ast.name: description
ast.source: If set to `true`, the input field will be a multiline textarea. Defaults to `false`.
ast.target: If set to `true`, the input field will be a multiline textarea. Defaults to `false`.

ast.name: description
ast.source: Text placed at the top of the modal.
ast.target: Text placed at the top of the modal.

ast.name: description
ast.source: This modules contains every internal function related to the web (making web requests).
ast.target: This modules contains every internal function related to the web (making web requests).

ast.name: description
ast.source: Retrieves and parses the daily quote from `https://github.com/Zachatoo/quotes-database` as a callout.
ast.target: Retrieves and parses the daily quote from `https://github.com/Zachatoo/quotes-database` as a callout.

ast.name: description
ast.source: Limits selection to photos matching a search term. Multiple search terms can be passed separated by a comma.
ast.target: Limits selection to photos matching a search term. Multiple search terms can be passed separated by a comma.

ast.name: description
ast.source: Optional argument to include the specified size in the image link markdown. Defaults to false.
ast.target: Optional argument to include the specified size in the image link markdown. Defaults to false.

ast.name: description
ast.source: Makes a HTTP request to the specified URL. Optionally, you can specify a path to extract specific data from the response.
ast.target: Makes a HTTP request to the specified URL. Optionally, you can specify a path to extract specific data from the response.

ast.name: description
ast.source: The URL to which the HTTP request will be made.
ast.target: The URL to which the HTTP request will be made.

ast.name: description
ast.source: A path within the response JSON to extract specific data.
ast.target: A path within the response JSON to extract specific data.

ast.target: Strip the Zettelkasten ID of title (if space separated)

ast.target: Suggester with mapping function (same as above example)
